meta.title: CFD Analysis | Novves
meta.description: NOVVES CFD (Computational Fluid Dynamics) analyses — real scenarios, real data, safe solutions.

hero.subtitle: Real Scenarios, Real Data, Safe Solutions.

cfdAnalizi.intro: At NOVVES, we don't just manufacture fans — we also provide project-specific, engineering-based ventilation solutions. At the core of these solutions lie CFD (Computational Fluid Dynamics) analyses that allow us to test our designs before implementing them on site.

whyCfd.title: Why CFD Analysis?
items: Fan system performance is simulated according to real environmental conditions.
Fire scenarios such as smoke, temperature and gas propagation are pre-tested.
Vital variables like visibility distance, temperature rise and CO concentration are analyzed.
Regulatory compliance and safe evacuation times are verified.
whyCfd.resultText: Correct placement, correct number of fans, optimum energy usage and maximum safety are ensured.

items.title: Air Velocity Distribution
items.subtitle: Velocity Analysis
items.text: The direction, speed and distribution of airflow within the enclosed space has been modeled in three dimensions. The aim is to ensure air circulation throughout the entire environment with the effect of jet fans.

items.title: Temperature Change
items.subtitle: Temperature Distribution
items.text: How the ambient temperature changes at human level height during a fire has been analyzed. Critical temperature limits have been evaluated and risks have been identified for emergency management.

items.title: Visibility Assessment
items.subtitle: Visibility Analysis
items.text: How long evacuation routes remain visible under heavy smoke has been analyzed. The rate of visibility reduction has been measured to verify evacuation safety.

items.title: Carbon Monoxide Dispersion
items.subtitle: CO Concentration
items.text: How exhaust gas CO accumulates over time in enclosed environments like parking garages and how it is evacuated by the fan system has been modeled.

items.title: Smoke Propagation
items.subtitle: Smoke Propagation
items.text: The direction and speed of smoke spread after a fire and how it is directed with the help of jet fans has been analyzed. Smoke layer formation and movement have been simulated in detail.

benefits.title: What You Gain with NOVVES
items: Pre-analysis of temperature, CO and visibility limits for evacuation safety
Clear visibility of system performance before installation
Project-based technical reporting and data-supported presentation
benefits.quote: Base your design not only on experience, but also on data. Build safe, powerful and optimized ventilation systems with NOVVES engineering.

hero.subtitle: We guarantee full performance operation of the system after installation.

intro: At Novves, we don't just produce quality products; we also guarantee full performance operation of the system after installation.
Our post-sales commissioning service is provided to ensure our customers use our products in the most efficient and safe way.

items.step: Product installation control and mechanical compliance inspection
Electrical connections and grounding compliance check
Initial fan startup and motor direction verification
Speed control device / frequency inverter settings configuration
Performing basic performance tests such as flow rate, pressure, temperature
Fan optimization according to ambient conditions
Brief training on system operation and basic maintenance information for the user
Recording all tests and procedures and reporting with commissioning form

meta.title: Smoke Control System Design | Novves
meta.description: Jet fan system design — air flow, exhaust extraction and smoke control in enclosed parking garages.

intro: The jet fan system is a mechanical ventilation system used to direct airflow, evacuate exhaust gases and control smoke in the event of a fire in enclosed parking garages.
This system has been developed by adapting tunnel ventilation technologies and is especially preferred in large and complex parking structures.

Jet fans create air movement over a wide area by blowing air at high speed, pushing and dragging the air mass in front of them. This way, harmful gases such as carbon monoxide (CO) from vehicles are effectively evacuated. During a fire, jet fans prevent the spread of smoke, ensuring the safety of escape routes and facilitating firefighter intervention.

items.title: Jet Fans
items.text: The main component of the system. Mounted on the ceiling. Creates airflow to direct polluted air to exhaust fans. Two types are available: axial and radial. Axial type can operate in single or dual direction.

items.title: Exhaust Fans
items.text: Expels the polluted or smoky air directed by jet fans to the outside in a controlled manner.

items.title: Fresh Air Fans
items.text: Used for pressurization to provide clean air in place of polluted air and to prevent smoke from reaching escape routes when natural openings are insufficient or not suitably positioned.

items.title: Sensors
items.text: CO, smoke and heat sensors collect data from their zone and transmit this information to the MCC panel according to set limits.

items.title: Automation and Control System (MCC Panel)
items.text: The system that enables automatic activation of fans based on data from sensors. During a fire, it ensures the system operates according to predetermined scenarios. PLC controlled and can be integrated with BMS (building management system).

advantages.title: System Advantages
advantages.subtitle: Jet fan systems offer significant advantages over traditional duct-type ventilation systems, especially in enclosed parking garages and tunnel systems.

items.title: No duct requirement
items.text: Jet fan systems work by directing air, so there is no need for air ducts, grilles or diffusers.

items.title: Space saving
items.text: In parking garages with low ceiling heights, duct systems can cause problems as they take up space. Jet fans take up less space with their compact dimensions.

items.title: Energy efficiency
items.text: Working with CO sensors, they only operate when needed, reducing energy consumption. Fans can run at variable speeds, providing optimized energy usage.

items.title: Smoke and heat extraction is under control
items.text: Operating at high speed during a fire, it controls the spread of smoke. It prevents escape routes from filling with smoke, ensuring life safety. It facilitates firefighter intervention.

items.title: Low maintenance cost
items.text: Without a duct system, cleaning and maintenance is easier. Fewer parts also reduces the risk of failure.

items.title: Easily positioned according to architecture
items.text: Redesigning the system for future changes is simpler.

items.title: Low initial investment cost
items.text: Since less equipment (ducts, grilles, diffusers, etc.) is used, the initial installation cost is lower compared to traditional systems.

designFactors.title: Important Design Factors
items: The necessary precautions for efficient system operation are carefully controlled and designed from the first stage, and placement is installed considering all conditions.

In jet fan projects, important factors such as noise level, required air direction planning, absence of "dead zones" where fresh air cannot reach, ease of installation, and accessibility of evacuation and escape routes during a fire are considered in the placement of axial fans (smoke and heat extraction / fresh air) and jet fans.

In jet fan system design, adequate air change rates and air flow must be provided considering vehicle exhaust gases, pedestrian traffic and other factors. The correct position of jet fans must be placed to direct the system's airflow in the most efficient way.

meta.title: On-Site Survey | Novves
meta.description: NOVVES on-site survey service - site inspection, measurement and analysis for project-specific ventilation solutions.

hero.subtitle: The first step to accurate project design: on-site measurement, analysis and needs assessment.

yerindeKesif.intro: Accurate data is at the foundation of every ventilation project. NOVVES on-site survey service is the first and most critical step in designing the most efficient and regulation-compliant solution by examining your project needs on-site. Our expert engineers visit your facility to perform detailed measurement, observation and analysis.

items.title: Appointment and Preliminary Information
items.desc: We learn the general characteristics of your project. We conduct a preliminary meeting for facility type, area size and needs analysis.

items.title: Site Visit and Measurement
items.desc: Our expert team visits the site to examine the space, take measurements and evaluate the existing infrastructure. Airflow directions, temperature distribution and current system status are analyzed.

items.title: Technical Analysis and Reporting
items.desc: Ventilation requirement calculations, flow rate analyses and regulation compliance checks are performed on the collected data. A detailed survey report is prepared.

items.title: Solution Proposal and Project Design
items.desc: Based on survey data, a project-specific proposal is presented with the most suitable system design, equipment selection and cost analysis.

items.title: Approval and Implementation Start
items.desc: After approving your proposal, production, installation and commissioning processes are planned and put into practice.

items.title: Accurate Design
items.desc: Equipment sizing errors are inevitable in projects done without on-site measurement.

items.title: Cost Optimization
items.desc: Oversized or undersized equipment selection is prevented with real site data.

items.title: Regulatory Compliance
items.desc: Compliance of existing building conditions with regulatory requirements is verified on-site.

items.title: Integration Ease
items.desc: Solutions compatible with existing mechanical and electrical infrastructure can only be determined through a survey.

cta.title: Request an on-site survey for your project
cta.subtitle: Our expert engineering team visits your facility to perform needs analysis and measurements.
